reforms.description: The Australian government has established the Building Australia Fund to finance infrastructure projects, transitioning to the Regional Infrastructure Fund funded by a new federal tax on mining profits. Additionally, Infrastructure Australia was created to advise on infrastructure policy and conduct audits for public and private investment needs. A major reform project has also been initiated to develop a National Broadband Network (NBN), replacing the existing copper telecommunications network with a fibre optic network managed by a public enterprise to ensure fair access for private operators.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance infrastructure, which is critical for economic growth and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into action as part of the government's infrastructure strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Building Australia Fund and the National Broadband Network represent significant structural changes in infrastructure policy.
reforms.source_quote: The total cost of this projects is expected to reach AUD43 billion, with public investment expected to total AUD26 billion over eight years.
